highlights.text: I’d be watching the reaction there and if we can flush it down to 5870 and recover, this would be ideal.” and “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.

It is also important to remember how ES institutions buys dips: Specifically, they put in Failed Breakdowns (my core setup): They find a low (usually the prior days low), flush it, grab liquidity/trap shorts, then price rallies.
Last week, ES settled into a sideways consolidation and as a result, we saw multiple Failed Breakdowns everyday.
5925 is a magnet in the middle.” As a result, the task for bulls generally everyday has been the same: Wait for the trap/Failed Breakdown.

Instead of bidding it, the obvious setup is to wait for it to test then recover today’s daily low which is at 5884 now.” At 8:15AM Friday, we saw exactly this and tested 5877, recovered 5884, then rallied to 5920-25.
That took us into today, and it would be the same script: Bulls would need to wait for the daily dip/Failed Breakdown which was again, below 5877.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep and educational dive into some of the quality Failed Breakdowns we had late last week (these were all provided to readers in advance), then I’ll discuss the actionable trade plan for tomorrow.

post_title: SPX Has Been Building A Pattern For 3 Weeks. Is It Ready To Break? June 3rd Plan
